findings.problem: The integrated docs repeatedly point readers at `old_2/project/code/motor.cpp` for launch, soft-start, stop, and scene-speed scheduling behavior, but those lifecycle semantics live in `old_2/project/code/isr.cpp` and `old_2/project/user/main.cpp`. `motor.cpp` mainly carries speed PID and steering control logic.
findings.evidence: `new/docs/race-finish-series.zh-CN/02-phase-b-low-speed-vehicle-motion.md:30` and `:94` assign soft-start and scene-speed behavior to `motor.cpp`, and `new/docs/race-finish-series.zh-CN/06-implementation-and-verification-contract.md:100` groups `motor.cpp` with runtime lifecycle semantics. In `old_2/project/code/isr.cpp:83-103`, `speed_target` switches across straight/circle guidance states; in `old_2/project/code/isr.cpp:162-191`, `run_stop`, `run_start`, and `run_brushless_start` implement stop and brushless PWM ramp-up; in `old_2/project/user/main.cpp:184-219`, key launch resets runtime state and enters `run_brushless_start`. By contrast, `old_2/project/code/motor.cpp:137-260` is the speed/steering PID surface and does not own that startup/stop state machine.
findings.recommendation: Retarget these references so lifecycle and scene-speed scheduling semantics point primarily to `old_2/project/code/isr.cpp` and `old_2/project/user/main.cpp`, while keeping `old_2/project/code/motor.cpp` scoped to motor PID and steering-control semantics.
